SessionStart.instruction: Read your native Claude Code project memory. Claude Code auto-injects it into context via system-reminder — it contains MEMORY.md (index + quick facts) and topic files (active-prs, cypress-baseline, api-migration, etc.). Read them fully before doing anything else. Do NOT look for or create AgentMemory.md.

UserPromptSubmit.instruction: Remember the skills available to you. Manage your tokens and delegate 'dumb' coding tasks to codex and 'dumb' research tasks to kiro when you can.

TaskCompleted.instruction: Update the native Claude Code project memory files (~/.claude/projects/<encoded-project>/memory/). Check off completed todos in MEMORY.md and append any new memories, user guidance, or notes worth preserving to the relevant topic file. Keep entries lean: only write what a future session would genuinely need after compaction.

Stop.instruction: Before stopping, update the native Claude Code project memory files (~/.claude/projects/<encoded-project>/memory/). Check off completed todos in MEMORY.md and append any new memories, user guidance, or notes worth preserving to the relevant topic file. Keep entries lean: only write what a future session would genuinely need after compaction.

SessionEnd.instruction: Before the session ends: 1. Slack — always post to channel C0APNMPDUJ3 with a short idle notice in the format '[Repo - Branch] - Agent Idle' followed by one-liner bullet points of todos completed this session, no other details. 2. Notion — review what was done this session, draft specific proposed updates (pages to create or fields to change), and ask the user to confirm before applying. 3. Wrench-DNA — review what was done this session, draft specific proposed changes (files to add or update, with a one-line summary of each), and ask the user to confirm before committing. 4. Native project memory — always update regardless (~/.claude/projects/<encoded-project>/memory/).
